meta.title: Deep Mastery Plan
meta.subtitle: v3 · Course Edition — Backend / Fullstack Systems Engineer

checkpoint.label: interview-ready as a mid-level fullstack Go/Node engineer

dailyRhythm.what: First thing, fresh brain. Udemy math course of your current level (sequence below). Watch → pause → solve by hand → check. Applies every single day of all 364 — it is not repeated in the day blocks.
NeetCode/LeetCode, unchanged. Timed problems, current pattern, always out loud on the last one.
The module's mini-build project (CodeCrafters where applicable), unchanged from v2.
Replaces "Reading." The module's Udemy course, sections mapped per day below. Watch at 1.25–1.5x, handwritten notes, re-draw one diagram from memory after each session.
Big build block (push the mini-project forward hard) + weekly review + 35m math review of the week.
Finish course sections, plan next week, re-solve failed DSA, math spaced-review set (redo 5 problems from two weeks ago).

weeklyNonNegotiables: Math: 35 min daily in the Udemy sequence + Sunday spaced-review set. Never traded away for build time.
One DSA problem per day solved out loud (NeetCode/LeetCode, unchanged).
No passive watching: every course session ends with notes, a redrawn diagram, or code — otherwise it counts as zero.
Every build ends with a README you could defend in an interview.
Saturday = biggest build push. Sunday = lighter: review, plan, backfill.
From Week 30 (Day 210) onward: at least one mock or real interview per week.

builds.title: TCP + HTTP server from raw sockets
Concurrent web crawler — worker pool, rate limiting, pprof
Production-style REST API — Postgres (pgx) + migrations + JWT + tests + Docker + CI
Fullstack service — TS + Fastify/NestJS BFF + thin React frontend, over build #6
SQLite clone — reads a real DB file, B-tree, SQL parsing
Database from scratch — disk B-tree + KV + WAL + transactions
Redis clone — RESP, event loop, persistence, replication
Kafka clone — wire protocol, partitions, offsets
Mini message broker — segmented log + consumer groups (extends #12)
Mini container runtime — namespaces + cgroups + chroot
Capstone — a distributed system that uses all of the above

modules.title: Setup & Baseline
modules.summary: Clean dev environment + honest starting point in DSA and math. Save every course from the shopping list into your Udemy library this week.

courses.title: Linux Mastery: Master the Linux Command Line
courses.author: Ziyad Yehia

blocks.text: 1st random NeetCode baseline problem, timed, record results.
Set up Linux dev box (WSL2/native), dotfiles, tmux, shell environment.
Placement: Krista King Fundamentals of Math — blitz the section quizzes to find where you stop scoring ~80%. (Optionally: one free Khan Academy course challenge as a 30-min diagnostic.)
Linux Mastery — shell + environment sections while you set up the box.
2nd baseline problem, timed.
Configure debuggers — gdb and delve working cleanly on sample programs.
Finish placement → lock your starting course & section.
Linux Mastery — processes, permissions, piping sections.
3rd baseline problem, timed.
Advanced Git fluency: interactive rebase, bisect, reflog.
Daily 35m begins at your placed section. From here it runs silently every day.
Finish the Linux Mastery skim.
4th baseline problem, timed.
Create master GitHub repos for all future mini-builds. Define clean templates.
Open Udemy Business → search the full shopping list (last page) → save every course; note any missing from the catalog and pick replacements now.
5th baseline problem, timed. Complete your baseline stats.
Create a CodeCrafters account. Skim all challenges, check C/Go language support.
Preview Module 1 courses: intros of Advanced C Programming and Operating Systems from Scratch.
Write your strict "code rules" checklist (naming, function layout, error-handling policy) enforced on every build project.
First weekly review. Verify all setup boxes ticked. Math week-review (35m).
Plan Module 1 in detail. Re-solve any failed baseline DSA. Math spaced set.
Any unfinished Linux sections; confirm all Module-1 courses are saved.

modules.title: C & OS Internals
modules.summary: You cannot build a mini-Redis or mini-Docker without memory, processes, and syscalls.
modules.coreBuilds: Custom memory allocator (C) · custom shell — fork/exec/pipes (CodeCrafters, C) · stretch: fixed-size thread pool
modules.dsaTarget: Arrays & Hashing → Two Pointers → Sliding Window → Stack

courses.title: Linux Inter Process Communication (IPC) from Scratch in C
courses.author: Abhishek Sagar

blocks.text: Arrays & Hashing on NeetCode. Talk out loud on the final problem daily.
Experimental C programs with pointer arithmetic. Map process memory segments via /proc/[pid]/maps. Plan the allocator's free-list architecture.
Advanced C — pointers, pointer arithmetic, dynamic memory & memory-layout sections.
Foundation of the Custom Memory Allocator: malloc() via sbrk() + first-fit explicit free list.
OS from Scratch paging sections. Design free() with coalescing.
Two Pointers, medium tracks.
Implement free() + boundary tags for coalescing. Debug core dumps with gdb.
OS from Scratch — memory management, paging, page faults, TLB sections; Advanced C debugging sections.
Complete the allocator. README explaining the free list + interview answers. Push to GitHub.
Memory-virtualization recap from course notes. Prep process-creation exploration.
Sliding Window — dynamic windows, string challenges.
Start CodeCrafters Shell in C: REPL engine, command parsing, internal builtins.
OS from Scratch — processes, direct execution, scheduling sections.
External command execution: fork(), execvp(), waitpid().
Plan redirection + piping architecture. IPC course — file abstraction & pipe sections.
Stack patterns (Valid Parentheses, monotonic stack).
Redirection (>, <) via dup2(); multi-command pipelines (|) via pipe().
IPC course — pipes/FIFOs sections; Multithreading course — threads intro.
Finalize the Shell on CodeCrafters: quoting edge cases, multi-pipe streams, architectural README. Then multi-threaded C experiments: create races, fix them with pthread_mutex_t.
Concurrency course catch-up. Plan the thread pool.
Mixed review across all Module 1 patterns.
Fixed-size thread pool in C: synchronized task queue + mutex + condition variable.
Multithreading course — locks, condition variables, thread-pool pattern sections. (epoll/alternative I/O: preview only — Hussein Nasser closes it properly in Module 2.)
Stress-test the thread pool. Document threading + memory lessons from Module 1.
Clean up repos. Plan the Module 2 networking sandbox.

modules.title: Networking + HTTP From Scratch
modules.summary: OSI/TCP-IP, CIDR, 3-way handshake, congestion control, sockets API, HTTP/1.1→3, TLS, L4 vs L7.
modules.coreBuilds: TCP echo server + client (C & Go) · HTTP server (CodeCrafters) · DNS server (CodeCrafters, Go)

blocks.text: Binary Search (classic, variations, matrix searches).
Raw TCP echo server + client in C: socket(), bind(), listen(), accept().
Nasser, Network Eng — OSI vs TCP/IP, IP, UDP, TCP internals sections. (CIDR shaky? Bombal subnetting sections.)
Rewrite the echo system in Go; compare Go's abstractions with C's raw descriptors. Capture your own handshake in Wireshark.
TCP state transitions, flow-control windows, TIME_WAIT — Nasser's TCP deep-dive sections.
Linked List (reversals, cycle detection, merges).
Start CodeCrafters HTTP server: manual header/request parsing from raw byte streams, no libraries.
Nasser — HTTP/1.1 limits, keep-alive, head-of-line blocking sections.
Concurrent connection handling, routing, text + binary file streaming.
Nasser — HTTP/2 streams, frames, HPACK; the path to HTTP/3/QUIC.
Advanced linked list (merge K lists, LRU-style problems).
Start CodeCrafters DNS server in Go: read raw network byte headers, map binary representations.
DNS sections in Nasser + record types (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX). DNS is thin on Udemy — the CodeCrafters challenge itself is the course here.
Manual DNS response construction — question/answer record byte arrays via bit shifting.
Resolution chains, caching, TTL. Plan the stretch goal.
Mixed timed binary search + linked list sets.
Recursive DNS forwarding + full test coverage.
Nasser — TLS handshakes, CA hierarchies, cert validation chains.
Complete the DNS server. Document L4 vs L7 in the README.
Install Go profiling toolsets (pprof, trace). Plan the Go deep-dive.

modules.title: Go Deep: Runtime + Production Backend
modules.summary: Two halves: runtime internals, then a production-grade API — clones prove depth, this proves you can ship. L3 interviews test both.
modules.coreBuilds: Concurrent web crawler (#5) · production REST API — Go + Postgres (pgx) + migrations + JWT + tests + Docker + CI (#6)

courses.title: Backend Master Class [Golang + PostgreSQL + Kubernetes + gRPC]
courses.author: TECH SCHOOL

courses.title: Runtime internals — Go blog GC/scheduler posts + GopherCon talks

blocks.text: Tree fundamentals: recursive properties, in/pre/post-order traversals.
Web crawler core: extraction workers, safe link-storage maps, channels + select.
Sawler — goroutines, channels, select, race conditions. GopherCon: "The Scheduler Saga" (GMP).
Context cancellation, bounded worker pool, rate limiting.
Go memory model; run escape analysis (go build -gcflags="-m"), map stack vs heap. GopherCon: "Understanding Allocations".
Level-order traversal via queues; BST intro (lookups, insertions).
Benchmarks + pprof heap/CPU profiling of the crawler.
Go blog — tri-color mark-sweep GC posts; Sawler worker-pool sections.
Finalize crawler; record a full memory-trace performance profile in the README.
Pick the API domain (bookings / tasks / wallet). Sketch schema + endpoints.
BST mutations, validation, kth-smallest.
Scaffold the REST API: chi (or stdlib) routing, config, structured logging (slog), health endpoints.
Backend Master Class — project structure, handlers, middleware sections.
Postgres via pgx + migrations (golang-migrate). Schema with proper indexes.
BMC — DB/migration sections; index warm-up on use-the-index-luke.com (deep dive comes in Module 5).
Tree recursion patterns (LCA, diameter).
CRUD handlers, request validation, clean error handling, pagination.
BMC — CRUD, validation, error-handling sections.
JWT auth + middleware chain (auth, rate limit, request ID, recovery).
BMC — auth (JWT/PASETO) sections; graceful shutdown, context timeouts throughout the stack.
Mixed timed tree sets.
Table-driven unit tests + integration tests with testcontainers.
BMC — testing + CI sections.
Dockerfile, GitHub Actions CI, README with architecture diagram. This repo is your "I can ship" proof.
Whiteboard the GMP scheduler and GC from memory. Plan the Node module.

modules.title: Node.js / TypeScript Deep + Fullstack Layer
modules.summary: V8 pipeline, event-loop phases, micro vs macrotasks, libuv, streams/buffers, strict TypeScript.
modules.coreBuilds: Mini event loop in JS (#7) · TS Fastify/NestJS BFF + thin React frontend over the Go API (#8)

blocks.text: Tree DFS pathing, path sums.
Mini event loop in JS: distinct microtask + macrotask queues mirroring real execution order.
Weird Parts — execution contexts, closures, async; Node Core Concepts — event-loop phases, nextTick/setImmediate.
Extend to nested setImmediate / process.nextTick sequencing; verify against real Node output.
V8 pipeline: Ignition bytecode, TurboFan JIT (Node Core Concepts V8 sections).
Tree BFS patterns, explicit state tracking across depths.
Scaffold the TS service (NestJS — fits your depth and the local/EPAM market): strict tsconfig, schema validation, error boundaries.
Understanding TypeScript — narrowing, generics; NestJS Guide — setup, modules, validation sections.
Auth flow + typed API client for your Go API from Module 3 (this service is its BFF).
Node Core Concepts — libuv thread pool: what's truly async vs pooled.
Serialize/deserialize trees, advanced recursion.
BFF endpoints aggregating the Go API; stream handlers; fast serialization hooks.
Node Core Concepts — streams & buffers sections; NestJS Guide — interceptors/guards refresh.
Vitest test suite; contract tests against the Go API.
Understanding TypeScript — structural typing rules; compile pipeline.
Mixed timed tree sets (interview pacing).
Minimal React (Vite) frontend on the BFF: one list view, one form, one auth flow. Just enough to say "fullstack" honestly.
React — The Complete Guide — essentials only: state, effects, data fetching. Do not let a 60-hour course eat the module.
docker-compose for the full stack (React → BFF → Go API → Postgres). One-command demo.
Polish both repos. Plan the database module.

modules.title: Databases: SQL + Postgres Internals + Build a DB
modules.summary: CTEs/window functions, B-tree indexes, MVCC, WAL, VACUUM, query planner, EXPLAIN ANALYZE. L3 interviews will test SQL — drill 20m of real SQL daily inside the build block, against your Module-3 Postgres.
modules.coreBuilds: SQLite clone (CodeCrafters, #9) · disk-backed B-tree KV database + WAL (#10)

courses.title: SQL and PostgreSQL: The Complete Developer's Guide
courses.author: Stephen Grider

courses.title: use-the-index-luke.com
courses.note: stays — free, short, unmatched for index reasoning

blocks.text: Heap foundations: build heaps, extractions.
Start CodeCrafters SQLite clone in Go: map the file header + structure of a real .db file. SQL drill: Grider aggregation exercises.
Nasser DB Eng — B-tree & indexing sections; Grider — aggregations, CTEs, window functions.
Traverse database page schemas down to root B-tree leaves.
Index structures on use-the-index-luke.com.
K-way merge, top-K frequent.
Basic SQL parser: recognize SELECT parameters, map columns. Daily SQL drill continues.
Nasser — log-structured (LSM) vs page-oriented (B-tree) storage sections.
Link the parser to B-tree leaf reads — return real results for key queries.
Page layout constraints, cell pointer arrays (Nasser page-layout sections).
Mixed heaps → Intervals intro (Merge/Insert Intervals).
Edge cases + table-scan paths for non-indexed queries.
Nasser — database internals & row/page storage sections.
Finalize the SQLite clone; interactive testing loop on CodeCrafters.
Transaction theory basics (Nasser ACID sections) before building a write engine.

segments.label: Storage Engine From Scratch — holiday weeks; hours may dip, protect the daily streak

blocks.text: Interval scheduling and minimization.
Start the build-your-own.org database in Go: binary file format, raw disk paging.
Nasser — transactions & recovery sections.
Disk-persisted B-tree node splitting + merging.
Postgres storage: heap files, TOAST, page allocation (Nasser Postgres sections).

segments.label: Storage Engine From Scratch — holiday weeks; hours may dip, protect the daily streak

blocks.text: Mixed heap + interval sets under tight timing.
KV lookup, insertion, continuous updates on the disk-backed engine.
Nasser — MVCC, VACUUM, WAL sections.
Light Write-Ahead Log for crash-recovery consistency.
EXPLAIN ANALYZE paths, hash joins, index scans (Nasser query-planner sections).

segments.label: Storage Engine From Scratch — holiday weeks; hours may dip, protect the daily streak

blocks.text: Advanced multi-interval / scheduling problems.
Simple MVCC hooks; complete the database.
Re-watch key Nasser storage sections at 2x for reinforcement.
Full performance profile; document tradeoffs in the README.
Plan Redis: RESP protocol requirements.

modules.title: Redis Internals + Build a Redis
modules.summary: RESP, single-threaded I/O-multiplexing event loop, skiplists, RDB/AOF, fork copy-on-write, eviction, caching patterns.
modules.coreBuilds: Redis clone (CodeCrafters, #11): RESP parser, TTL expiry, RDB parsing, master-replica replication. Then wire cache-aside into your own Go API.

courses.title: RESP wire spec + redis.io persistence/replication docs
courses.note: gear 2 internals — short, free, better than any course for a clone

blocks.text: Backtracking: subsets, state-space tree drawings.
Start the Redis clone in Go: raw RESP parser (simple strings, errors, integers, bulk arrays).
RESP wire spec (redis.io) + Grider Redis — data-structure sections.
RESP parser → persistent TCP engine; command table for PING, SET, GET.
Why the single-threaded multiplexed event loop avoids locking bottlenecks.
Permutations generation and pruning.
TTL key-expiry manager: active + passive background scanners without blocking the loop.
Grider — expiration & key-management sections; redis.io hash-table/skiplist internals notes.
RDB snapshot parsing — reconstruct state on startup.
fork() copy-on-write during background RDB serialization.
Combination Sum variations, search-tree optimization.
Master-replica architecture: replica handshake, stream updates over master sockets.
redis.io replication docs; Grider — replication/cluster sections.
Continuous command-propagation engine to downstream replicas.
Cache invalidation: cache-aside, write-through, thundering herd, stampede control (Grider caching-pattern sections).
N-Queens, Word Search.
LRU/LFU eviction + memory constraints. Then: add a cache-aside layer to your Module-3 Go API — real integration, real README material.
Grider — eviction policies; whiteboard the full Redis data-path from memory.
Finalize the Redis clone; README covering RESP parsing + replication.
Prep Checkpoint Week: list what to polish, book two mock interviews.

modules.title: L3 Checkpoint
modules.summary: The 7-month milestone. You now have: OS + networking fundamentals, deep Go, deep Node/TS, a production Go API, a TS fullstack service, SQL/Postgres competence, a Redis clone with caching integrated, and DSA through backtracking. That is a legitimate mid-level fullstack Go/Node profile.

blocks.text: Polish flagship repo #6 (Go API): README, architecture diagram, one-command demo, CI badge.
Polish flagship repo #8 (TS fullstack): same treatment. Pin both + best clones on GitHub profile.
CV rewrite: lead with builds ("Implemented a Redis-compatible server with master-replica replication in Go"). Update LinkedIn.
Mock interview #1 (Pramp/peer): DSA, timed, out loud.
Mock interview #2: practical fullstack — API design, SQL, live debugging.
System-design lite: whiteboard a URL shortener and a rate limiter end to end (enough for mid-level screens).

Honest gap list. Decision: start applying now and run the rest of the plan in parallel (recommended — real interviews are the best training), or push to the capstone first. From here: at least one mock or real interview per week.

modules.title: Messaging: Kafka + RabbitMQ + Build a Broker
modules.summary: Log-centric append-only design, partitions, offsets, ISR, delivery semantics, zero-copy, AMQP exchanges/bindings, push vs pull.
modules.coreBuilds: Kafka clone (CodeCrafters, #12) · segmented-log broker + consumer groups (#13) · mini AMQP broker (#14)

courses.title: Apache Kafka Series — Learn Apache Kafka for Beginners v3
courses.author: Stéphane Maarek

courses.title: Apache Kafka Series — Kafka Cluster Setup & Administration
courses.author: Maarek

courses.title: Top-rated practical RabbitMQ course from your catalog
courses.note: AMQP model is standard across all of them; you know RabbitMQ from Ucell production — this formalizes it

blocks.text: Graph structures: matrices, adjacency lists, DFS traversals.
Start the Kafka clone in Go: binary protocol parser for ApiVersions requests.
Maarek Beginners — topics, partitions, brokers, producers/consumers sections.
Decode Produce and Fetch binary frames.
Zero-copy (sendfile) and how Kafka bypasses userspace (free Confluent deep-dive talk).
BFS shortest paths.
Extend into a real segmented log storage engine: binary segments on disk + index trackers.
Maarek — delivery semantics, replayability, consumer-group sections.
Consumer offset management + consumer group registration tables.
ISR tracking, KRaft control plane (Cluster Setup & Administration sections).
Topological sort, dependency scheduling.
Complete the Kafka-compatible broker; end-to-end streaming integration tests.
Maarek — idempotent producers, exactly-once configuration sections.
High-quality README: binary framing + log structure.
Switch to push-based routing; sketch the AMQP broker layout.
Union-Find / Disjoint Set.
Mini AMQP broker in Go: Direct, Fanout, and Topic exchanges.
RabbitMQ course — exchanges, bindings, queues sections.
Dynamic in-memory binding tables; wildcard routing tokens.
Backpressure, prefetch, consumer push-loop bottlenecks (RabbitMQ course QoS sections).
Cycle detection, islands.
Consumer acknowledgments + dead-letter queue routing.
RabbitMQ course — acks & DLQ sections; whiteboard Kafka vs RabbitMQ use cases cold.
Wrap the AMQP broker; document the design.
Prep the RPC/proxy playground for Module 9.

modules.title: gRPC + Nginx/Envoy + Build a Proxy
modules.summary: Protobuf wire layout (varints, wire types), HTTP/2 multiplexing, load-balancing algorithms, health checks, Envoy xDS.
modules.coreBuilds: gRPC Go services (unary + bidi streaming, interceptors) · custom reverse proxy / load balancer (#15)

courses.title: gRPC [Golang] Master Class: Build Modern API & Microservices
courses.author: Stéphane Maarek

courses.title: NGINX Fundamentals: High Performance Servers from Scratch
Envoy/xDS
courses.note: no strong Udemy course — official Envoy docs + "Life of a Request in Envoy" talk

blocks.text: Dijkstra shortest paths.
Protobuf service contracts; unary + bidirectional streaming RPCs in Go (these become your capstone contracts).
Maarek gRPC — protobuf, varints & wire types, unary/streaming sections.
Interceptor middleware: deadlines, timeouts, metadata.
HTTP/2 transport internals: framing, flow-control windows, stream multiplexing (revisit Nasser's HTTP/2 sections).
MST (Prim's / Kruskal's).
Start the reverse proxy / load balancer in Go: low-level HTTP request forwarding.
NGINX Fundamentals — reverse-proxy architecture, connection pooling sections.
Round-Robin + Weighted Least-Connections across live targets.
Circuit breaking, backpressure, passive health scans (NGINX LB sections).
2D DP intro: grid pathing.
Active health checks + retry loops; graceful target-pool recovery.
Envoy xDS control plane docs; sidecar mesh paradigm ("Life of a Request in Envoy").
Complete the LB; benchmark under simulated backend failures.
Install namespace/cgroup tooling for Module 10.

modules.title: Containers, Kubernetes, Observability
modules.summary: Linux namespaces, cgroups, chroot, OCI/runc, K8s control plane, PromQL, distributed tracing.
modules.coreBuilds: Container runtime (CodeCrafters, #16) · instrument your clones + Go API with Prometheus and OpenTelemetry
modules.dsaTarget: DP deep dive (1D/2D, knapsack, LIS, edit distance)

courses.title: Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) with Practice Tests
courses.author: Mumshad Mannambeth
courses.note: the Kubernetes course; do it even if you never sit the exam

courses.title: OpenTelemetry
courses.note: catalog is young — any OTel course, or official OTel docs + demo app

blocks.text: 1D DP: sequence optimization.
Start CodeCrafters container runtime in Go: process isolation via exec.Command flags.
Docker Mastery — image layers, union filesystems sections.
Linux namespaces (PID, NET, MNT, UTS) via syscalls.
Cgroup filesystem trees (/sys/fs/cgroup) — man pages are the real course here.
1D DP: Longest Increasing Subsequence.
Cgroup CPU + memory enforcement on the runtime.
Man pages: chroot(), capabilities, seccomp; Docker Mastery internals sections for context.
Complete the runtime; run isolated sandbox shells from a raw root directory.
OCI/containerd model. Plan the K8s + observability half.
2D DP: knapsack.
Embed Prometheus metrics into your Redis + Kafka clones and Go API.
CKA (Mumshad) — architecture, reconciliation loops, scheduling, CNI sections + labs.
OpenTelemetry tracing spans across services, down to the socket layer.
RED/USE patterns, SLI/SLO calculations (Prometheus course sections).
2D DP: edit distance.
Prometheus + Grafana + Jaeger stack; dashboards for your clones' telemetry.
Prometheus course — PromQL, alerting, Grafana sections; Linux perf tools (strace, lsof, iostat, vmstat) via man pages.
Verify metrics + spans under stress tests; telemetry writeup in the repo.
Prep system-design drills.

modules.title: System Design Deep
modules.summary: CAP/PACELC, sharding/partitioning, consistent hashing, Raft, replication topologies. Your edge: you've written the cache, the broker, and the LB everyone else only names.
modules.dsaTarget: Mixed timed sets across ALL patterns (strict interview simulation)

courses.title: ByteByteGo
courses.author: Alex Xu
courses.note: free YouTube — payment systems & event logs; no Udemy presence

blocks.text: Mixed Timed Set 1 (arrays, windows, trees).
Full whiteboard designs: URL shortener, rate limiter, news feed — with constraints and capacity math.
Frank Kane — rate limiters, consistent hashing sections.
Real-time chat architecture: connection management, state handling.
CAP/PACELC, sharding strategies, Raft (Nasser replication/sharding sections + a Raft visualization).
Mixed Timed Set 2 (graphs, heaps).
Distributed cache layer; search autocomplete infrastructure.
Frank Kane — caching, autocomplete, news-feed sections.
Payment system + high-throughput event log designs.
Consensus protocols recap; ByteByteGo payment/event-log videos.
Mixed Timed Set 3 (hard DP + backtracking).
Compile a personal design-notes repo; redraw every system from memory.
Re-watch weak-spot sections at 2x; ByteByteGo deep-dives.
Draw and defend any layout under pressure — test yourself cold.
Finalize deployment templates. Capstone starts tomorrow.

modules.title: Capstone + Interview Mode
modules.summary: Tie everything into a resilient, production-grade distributed system and switch to interview delivery mode. Capstone: 3 Go microservices over gRPC · Postgres · Redis cache-aside · Kafka backbone · your custom LB in front · Kubernetes · Prometheus + OTel · TS BFF + thin React on top. Standing rhythm: mock interviews Tue & Thu evenings; DSA = mixed timed sets, out loud. No course slot — the 30m goes to STAR-story rehearsal and mock-interview review.

blocks.text: Scaffold the 3 Go microservices; define gRPC contracts; wire inter-service pipelines.
Mocks Tue/Thu. Story work: draft STAR stories 1–5.
Postgres integration: migrations, indexing, connection pooling.
Verify data safety under simulated network-split tests.
Core business logic across services; Redis cache-aside layer.
Mocks Tue/Thu. STAR stories 6–10, mapped to competencies.
Kafka event backbone: producers, partition strategy.
Consumer offset design review; failure-mode notes.
Wire Kafka consumers end to end; partition-level offset tracking.
Mocks Tue/Thu.
Put your custom reverse proxy/LB in front of the services.
Performance analysis; profile memory under high-volume load.
Hardening: retries, idempotency keys, timeouts, chaos tests.
Rehearse STAR stories out loud — under 3 minutes each.
Containerize the entire topology.
Full docker-compose verification of every service path.
Deploy to Kubernetes; configure scaling boundaries and probes.
Heavy system-design mocks — leverage your internals knowledge as the edge.
Full monitoring suite: Prometheus collections, Grafana dashboards, OTel spans.
Verify service discovery + cross-node network metrics; SLO dashboards.
Connect the TS Node BFF + React front; end-to-end user-path verification.
Resume final pass: capstone on top, then mini-builds.
Load tests through the full path (proxy → services → Kafka/Redis/Postgres).
Fix everything the load tests exposed.
Rapid-fire mixed drills under strict timing.
Final documentation: architecture diagrams, live dashboards, runbooks.
Complete the capstone. Publish the live, production-monitored, beautifully documented codebase on GitHub.
Step back. Review the full portfolio: 17 builds, a distributed capstone, a year of math momentum. You are interview-ready far beyond the L3 bar. Ship it.
